title: festuca altaica var. parishii
intro: Festuca altaica var. parishii, also known as Parish's fescue, is a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Festuca altaica var. parishii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support digestive health. Traditional herbal preparations include teas, tinctures, and salves made from the plant's leaves and roots, which can be used to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
